title: AWS CloudWatch Integration Failing
content:
CloudDash connects to AWS CloudWatch using IAM role assumption with an external ID for security. Integration failures usually indicate IAM misconfiguration, expired credentials, or missing permissions.

Verify the IAM trust relationship: In your AWS account, locate the IAM role created for CloudDash (typically named CloudDashIntegrationRole). The trust policy must allow CloudDash's AWS account (ID: 123456789012) to assume the role with the external ID shown in CloudDash Settings > Integrations > AWS > Setup Instructions. The external ID is unique per organization and prevents confused deputy attacks.

Check attached permissions: The role needs CloudWatchReadOnlyAccess at minimum. For log ingestion, add a custom policy granting logs:FilterLogEvents, logs:GetLogEvents, and logs:DescribeLogGroups on your log group ARNs. For alarm synchronization, include cloudwatch:DescribeAlarms and cloudwatch:DescribeAlarmHistory.

Run the CloudDash connectivity test: Settings > Integrations > AWS > Test Connection. The test performs sts:AssumeRole, cloudwatch:ListMetrics, and logs:DescribeLogGroups calls. Each step reports pass/fail with specific error messages. Common errors include 'AccessDenied' (missing permissions), 'InvalidClientTokenId' (wrong role ARN), and 'ExternalIdMismatch' (external ID does not match trust policy).

Cross-account setups: If monitoring multiple AWS accounts, deploy the CloudDash integration role in each account and register them under Settings > Integrations > AWS > Linked Accounts. The primary account role needs sts:AssumeRole permission on linked account roles.

Metric gaps after successful connection: CloudWatch metrics with insufficient data points (less than 2 per evaluation period) appear as gaps in CloudDash. Verify the AWS resource is actively generating metrics and that metric retention (15 months for CloudWatch) has not expired for the requested time range.
